//notes: we assume that ESBI has the same thickness as ESAPI: 0.9 in.
we model this item as a 6″×8″ tall ESBI plate, which is one possible issued design
description: A small polygonal ballistic plate wrapped in a thin layer of nylon.  It combines a ceramic strike face with a polyethylene backer in order to achieve protection against even armor-piercing rifle rounds.  The ceramic breaks on impact, absorbing much of the damage, so while this plate is tough, it will wear out after a few hits.  Must be inserted into a plate carrier to be used.  As a side plate, it can only go into side-plate pockets, thus offering additional coverage for the torso.

variants.description:
The standard ballistic plate issued by the U.S. Army, ESBI -- Enhanced Side Ballistic Insert -- is a small full-cut ballistic plate with a single-curve profile, composed of a boron carbide strike face, a UHMWPE backer, and a nylon cover.  This plate has been certified ESBI, which is comparable with the civilian NIJ IV rating.  Plates like this one are intended to be inserted into a plate carrier, where it will absorb the energy of multiple rifle rounds before requiring replacement.  On the front, it says <color_yellow>
STRIKE FACE
HANDLE WITH CARE</color>
On the back, it says <color_yellow>
ESBI GEN II</color>, as well as the name of the manufacturer -- Leading Technology Composites, Inc. -- and its NSN.

//weight: Abrams store says one plate weighs between 990 and 995 g
we rely on the Army source, because it's more likely to be accurate
//damage_verb: makes a crunch, something has shifted
